ddls:
CREATE TABLE "signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal configuration */,
  "signal_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal */,
  "bits" INTEGER /* Number of bits for the signal */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the signal (e.g., TLX) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the signal capabilities */,
  "signal_group" TEXT /* Categorize signals into groups for better organization */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the signal is currently active */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the signal configuration was updated */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration was last updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the configuration */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the configuration */,
  "is_archived" INTEGER /* Indicates if the configuration is archived */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the configuration is used (e.g., test, production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "capabilities" (
  "capability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each capability entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated signal */,
  "capability_type" TEXT /* Type of capability (e.g., Template, Rate Limit) */,
  "capability_value" TEXT /* Value associated with the capability */,
  "is_mandatory" INTEGER /* Indicates if the capability is mandatory */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the capability was updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the capability */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("capability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_capabilities_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the system */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session ID for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each template */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the template */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the template */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the template is active */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the template was updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the template */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rate_limits" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rate limit entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated signal */,
  "rate_limit_value" TEXT /* Value of the rate limit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rate limit implementation */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the rate limit is the default for the signal */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the rate limit was updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the rate limit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rate_limits_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Details on what was changed in this version */,
  "is_released" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is released */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was released */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the version was updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "monitoring_data" (
  "monitor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each monitoring entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated signal */,
  "value" REAL /* Current value of the signal being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was recorded */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the monitoring data (e.g., sensor, API) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the monitoring data (e.g., normal, warning, error) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the monitoring data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("monitor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_data_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the notification (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the notification (e.g., system, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
